summary: Converted Target Test Mewtwo stage code from a standalone assembly file into `src/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.c`, reaching a reported 69.69% file match. The PR added typed StageData/StageCallbacks setup for `grTMewtwo`, switched the build from `asm/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.s.o` to `src/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.c.o`, and renamed several `StageInfo` offset fields from raw `x6A8`-style names to archive-derived names such as `itemdata`, `coll_data`, `param`, `map_ptcl`, `map_texg`, `yakumono_param`, `map_plit`, and `quake_model_set`. Some functions remained inline `ASM`/`NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks, especially the large `grTMewtwo_802224B4` map/animation lookup and simple bool-return callbacks.
what_changed: Deleted the 379-line standalone assembly file `asm/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.s` and added a 328-line C translation at `src/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.c`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` so `grtmewtwo` builds from `src/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.c.o` instead of `asm/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.s.o`.
Defined `grTMewtwo_803E8F70` as a `StageCallbacks` array and `grTMewtwo_803E8FCC` as the stage descriptor for `/GrTMt.dat`.
Translated many small stage lifecycle/callback functions into C, including setup, spawn, animation init, cleanup/update wrappers, and trivial return/empty callbacks.

Kept several functions as inline `ASM` guarded by `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` with `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks, including `grTMewtwo_80222398`, `grTMewtwo_802223F8`, `grTMewtwo_80222488`, `grTMewtwo_802224B4`, and `grTMewtwo_802225C8`.
Renamed `StageInfo` archive/data pointer fields from offsets to semantic names in `types.h` and updated uses across `grdatfiles.c`, `ground.c`, and `grlib.c`.
Changed `Ground_801C49F8` to return `stage_info.yakumono_param`, matching the renamed field previously known as `x6C0`.
Updated `tools/convert_data.py` default `input_glob` from `**/grtpeach.s` to `**/grtmewtwo.s`, indicating this PR used the data conversion helper for the target file.
smart_moves: Used archive public-address strings from `grDatFiles_801C6038` as evidence for naming `StageInfo` fields, e.g. `coll_data`, `grGroundParam` -> `param`, `itemdata`, `map_ptcl`, `map_texg`, `yakumono_param`, `map_plit`, and `quake_model_set`.
Converted the stage descriptor and callback table into typed C data instead of leaving `.4byte` data in assembly, making the stage registration searchable and reusable.
Renamed the erroneous or inherited `grTMars`-style symbol family to `grTMewtwo_*`, and updated `ground.c` extern/table references from `grTMt_803E8FCC` to `grTMewtwo_803E8FCC`.
Preserved difficult unmatched regions with inline `ASM` under `MUST_MATCH` instead of blocking the whole file conversion, allowing partial C progress while keeping matching behavior available.
Used existing stage macros and types such as `Ground_GObj`, `GET_GROUND`, `GET_JOBJ`, `StageCallbacks`, and `StageData` to express common ground-stage patterns cleanly.
decomp_lessons: When a stage assembly file contains both code and data, first convert the callback table and `StageData` descriptor; this unlocks better function prototypes and makes cross-stage comparisons easier.
Archive key strings passed to `HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress` are strong evidence for naming `StageInfo` fields and should be propagated through users rather than keeping raw offset names.

Stage test-character files can share boilerplate: create ground GObjs with `Ground_801C14D0`, clear `x8_callback`/`xC_callback`, attach `grDisplay_801C5DB0` through `GObj_SetupGXLink`, call callback0 immediately, and install callback2 through `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54` when present.

Large switch/lookup logic involving `mpLib_80056B6C`, `mpLib_80054C6C`, and per-surface animation tables may be left inline temporarily if it is the main unmatched blocker; smaller lifecycle functions can still be matched independently.

Renaming shared struct fields may cause broad but mechanical diffs in core ground code; keep those changes semantically tied to clear evidence to help reviewers and future decompilers trust the rename.
naming_conventions: `grTMewtwo_<address>` was used for static functions and globals in the converted Target Test Mewtwo file.
`grTMewtwo_803E8F70` names the stage callback table and `grTMewtwo_803E8FCC` names the exported `StageData` descriptor.

`StageInfo` fields at offsets `+6A8` through `+6CC` were renamed to archive/data-purpose names: `itemdata`, `coll_data`, `param`, `ald_yaku_all`, `map_ptcl`, `map_texg`, `yakumono_param`, `map_plit`, and `quake_model_set`.
The new header uses the address-based include guard `GALE01_2221D8`, matching the address of the first function in the file.
Local unknown structure naming remained conservative: `grTMewtwo_UnkStruct` with field `x0`, avoiding overclaiming layout details.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the standalone `.s` file from the object list and replaced it with a C object to allow mixed C/inline-ASM progress.
Used `ASM bool ... #if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP) { NOT_IMPLEMENTED; } #else { nofralloc ... }` for unmatched functions, preserving exact assembly in matching builds.

Retained exact inline assembly for trivial bool-return functions even when they are only 8 bytes, likely because normal C did not yet satisfy the matching constraints or the project convention allowed placeholder ASM.
Represented the stage flags from raw `.4byte` constants as bit expressions such as `(1 << 0) | (1 << 2) | (1 << 4) | (1 << 5)` and `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)`.
The generated C includes a comment in `grTMewtwo_80222284`: `@todo Can't move below callbacks`, documenting an ordering/matching sensitivity around the `gobj` local and callback pointer setup.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `grTMewtwo_802224B4` or remove its inline ASM block.
Search for more `StageInfo` offset-field renames around `x6A8`, `x6AC`, `x6B0`, `x6C0`, and `x6CC` to confirm naming consistency.
Compare `grTMewtwo_80222284` against other Target Test stage spawn functions for callback table boilerplate reuse.
Look for later cleanup of `tools/convert_data.py` default `input_glob` after this targeted conversion.
